task_completion.description: Load a sample HAWT project into the simulation software
task_completion.visual_evidence: The QBlade GUI is open and displays a loaded HAWT (Horizontal Axis Wind Turbine) project, likely accessed from /home/ga/Documents/sample_projects/.

task_completion.description: Conduct a BEM simulation with a Tip Speed Ratio (TSR) of 7-8
task_completion.visual_evidence: A BEM (Blade Element Momentum) simulation module or results screen is visible in QBlade, showing the Tip Speed Ratio (TSR or lambda) parameter set to a value between 7 and 8.

task_completion.description: Perform a BPM noise simulation with specific observer coordinates
task_completion.visual_evidence: The BPM (Brooks-Pope-Marcolini) aeroacoustic simulation module is active. The observer position settings must be visible and set to 500m downwind (distance) and 2m (height).

task_completion.description: Export the noise spectrum to noise_spectrum_output.txt
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog exporting to /home/ga/Documents/noise_spectrum_output.txt, or a file manager/text editor showing this file containing tabular data with Frequency and SPL (Sound Pressure Level) columns.

task_completion.description: Save the complete project as turbine_noise_analysis.wpa
task_completion.visual_evidence: The QBlade window title shows 'turbine_noise_analysis.wpa', or a file manager shows the file 'turbine_noise_analysis.wpa' successfully created in /home/ga/Documents/projects/.

integrity.description: Agent used the QBlade GUI to perform the simulations
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent clicking through the QBlade interface to set up the BEM and BPM simulations, rather than just creating mock files using terminal commands or a text editor.

integrity.description: The exported noise spectrum contains genuine simulation data
integrity.visual_evidence: If the contents of noise_spectrum_output.txt are visible, it should contain realistic numerical data for Frequency (Hz) and SPL (dB/dBA), not placeholder text or manually typed fake numbers.

integrity.description: The project file was saved via the application
integrity.visual_evidence: The .wpa file should be generated by using the 'Save' or 'Save As' function within QBlade, not by using the 'touch' command or echoing text into a file with a .wpa extension.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The software used for this task is QBlade, an open-source wind turbine simulation tool. QBlade uses the .wpa extension for its project files. The BPM (Brooks-Pope-Marcolini) model is a standard aeroacoustic prediction method available in QBlade. The target Tip Speed Ratio (TSR) of 7-8 is realistic (optimal for the NREL 5MW turbine is ~7.55). The exported noise spectrum should be a text file (.txt) containing tabular data mapping Frequency (Hz) to Sound Pressure Level (SPL or dBA). Observer settings should explicitly show 500m distance and 2m height in the acoustic module configuration.
